message:

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "dapr-4233-outbox-pattern-for-statestore-pubsub-in-dapr",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "dapr: Outbox pattern for statestore + pubsub in Dapr",
+    "description": "Outbox pattern for statestore + pubsub in Dapr. Requested by 31+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current dapr deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your dapr version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr\nhelm list -n dapr 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working dapr installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review dapr configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant dapr configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr\nkubectl get configmap -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dapr\n```\n## Overview\n\nHi guys. Maybe Dapr could provide an outbox & inbox infrastructure since any distributed app development framework needs it to make sure domain events are published and subscribed."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Outbox pattern for statestore + pubsub in Dapr",
+        "description": "Outbox doesn't need time based scheduling, but where this might make a lot of sense is for delayed pubsub scheduling. The only issue I see at the moment for using actor reminders to schedule pub/sub activities is the RPS limitation that is imposed based on the current design. This leads back to our\n```yaml\napiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1\nkind: Component\nmetadata:\n  name: statestore\nspec:\n  type: state.mongodb\n  version: v1\n  - name: outboxPublishPubsub # Required\n    value: mypubsub\n  - name: outboxPublishTopic # Required\n    value: orders\n  - name: outboxPubsub # Optional\n    value: myoutboxpubsub\n  - name: outboxStateScanDelay # Optional\n    value: 5s\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr\nkubectl get events -n dapr --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Outbox pattern for statestore + pubsub in Dapr\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Outbox doesn't need time based scheduling, but where this might make a lot of sense is for delayed pubsub scheduling. The only issue I see at the moment for using actor reminders to schedule pub/sub activities is the RPS limitation that is imposed based on the current design. This leads back to our discussion on improving the actor reminder system.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1\nkind: Component\nmetadata:\n  name: statestore\nspec:\n  type: state.mongodb\n  version: v1\n  - name: outboxPublishPubsub # Required\n    value: mypubsub\n  - name: outboxPublishTopic # Required\n    value: orders\n  - name: outboxPubsub # Optional\n    value: myoutboxpubsub\n  - name: outboxStateScanDelay # Optional\n    value: 5s"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
